title: Unblock the merge loop: untrack the log files that keep main permanently dirty, and serialize verify
action: Two open P1s, both repo-level. (1) `.bee/logs/{dispatch,hooks,tools}.jsonl` are tracked in git despite .gitignore listing `.bee/logs/` - committed before the ignore existed. Every bee tool call rewrites them, `bee worktree merge` refuses on a dirty main, and the merge role must run in main, so it dirties main with its own first command and then refuses its own merge every cycle forever. Remove them from the index only (`git rm --cached`), never from disk; make sure the ignore actually covers them afterwards. (2) D12's cross-process verify lock was never built: under D5 the configured verify can run in four worktrees plus a fifth inside `bee worktree merge`, on ~5GB free, producing OOM-driven red verifies indistinguishable from real failures. Wrap the recorded `commands.verify` in a flock on a fixed path shared by main and every worktree (a path OUTSIDE any checkout, e.g. /home/vantt/projects/.herdr-orchestrating-verify.lock), using `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs config set` - never hand-edit .bee/config.json. Also fix the duplicate PBI-031 and PBI-032 rows in docs/backlog.md (each appears twice with different statuses; keep the correct one).

truths: The three log files still exist on disk
commands.verify is wrapped in a flock whose lock path lies outside every checkout
The wrapped verify still runs the same underlying commands
docs/backlog.md has exactly one row per PBI id

artifacts.substantive: commands.verify serialized behind a shared flock

prohibitions: Do not delete the log files from disk - index-only removal
Do not hand-edit .bee/config.json; use the config CLI
Do not change what the verify actually runs, only serialize it

verify: set -e; test -z "$(git ls-files .bee/logs | grep '\.jsonl$' || true)"; test -f .bee/logs/tools.jsonl; node -e "const c=require('./.bee/config.json');const v=c.commands.verify;if(!/flock/.test(v)){console.error('verify not serialized: '+v);process.exit(1)}if(!/cargo test/.test(v)||!/npm run test/.test(v)){console.error('verify lost commands: '+v);process.exit(1)}const m=v.match(/flock\s+(\S+)/);if(!m||m[1].includes('herdr-gateway--wt--')){console.error('lock path is inside a checkout: '+(m&&m[1]));process.exit(1)}"; test -z "$(grep -oE '^\| PBI-[0-9]+' docs/backlog.md | sort | uniq -d)"; echo MERGE_UNBLOCKED

trace.outcome: Main can be clean again, verify runs one at a time, and the backlog has one row per PBI - the three repo-level conditions the merge loop needs to work at all.
trace.verify_command: set -e; test -z "$(git ls-files .bee/logs | grep '\.jsonl$' || true)"; test -f .bee/logs/tools.jsonl; node -e "const c=require('./.bee/config.json');const v=c.commands.verify;if(!/flock/.test(v)){console.error('verify not serialized: '+v);process.exit(1)}if(!/cargo test/.test(v)||!/npm run test/.test(v)){console.error('verify lost commands: '+v);process.exit(1)}const m=v.match(/flock\s+(\S+)/);if(!m||m[1].includes('herdr-gateway--wt--')){console.error('lock path is inside a checkout: '+(m&&m[1]));process.exit(1)}"; test -z "$(grep -oE '^\| PBI-[0-9]+' docs/backlog.md | sort | uniq -d)"; echo MERGE_UNBLOCKED
